title: Add end-to-end Telegram image intake (photo + image document) into chat pipeline
summary: Implement native Telegram inbound image handling so users can send photos/screenshots directly to Prom and have them processed in the same chat flow. This removes the current text-only gate in telegram-channel polling and message handling.
details:
## Why this change
Inbound Telegram handling is currently text-only in two places, which prevents users from sending images for analysis:
- `src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts:550-553` only routes updates when `update.message?.text` exists.
- `src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts:2285-2287` returns early unless `msg.text` exists.
As a result, image-only messages (Telegram `photo` array or image `document`) are dropped before they ever reach `handleChat`.

This patch adds full, deterministic image intake for Telegram photos and image documents, while preserving all existing command and text behavior.

## Exact source edits
### 1) Extend Telegram update typings to include media fields
File: `src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts`
- In `interface TelegramUpdate` message shape (currently around lines `157-166`), add:
  - `caption?: string`
  - `photo?: Array<{ file_id: string; file_unique_id: string; width: number; height: number; file_size?: number }>`
  - `document?: { file_id: string; file_unique_id: string; file_name?: string; mime_type?: string; file_size?: number }`

### 2) Add helpers for Telegram file resolution/download
File: `src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts`
- Add private helper methods in `TelegramChannel`:
  - `private isImageDocument(doc: { mime_type?: string; file_name?: string } | undefined): boolean`
    - Returns true for `mime_type` starting with `image/` OR filename extensions `png|jpg|jpeg|webp|gif|bmp`.
  - `private async getTelegramFilePath(fileId: string): Promise<string>`
    - Calls `this.apiCall('getFile', { file_id: fileId })` and returns `result.file_path`.
    - Throws explicit error if missing path.
  - `private async downloadTelegramFileAsDataUrl(filePath: string): Promise<{ dataUrl: string; mimeType: string; sizeBytes: number }>`
    - Fetches `https://api.telegram.org/file/bot${this.config.botToken}/${filePath}`
    - Reads bytes into `Buffer`, infers mime from extension when needed, emits `data:image/...;base64,...`.

### 3) Allow poll loop to process non-text messages
File: `src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts`
- In `pollLoop()` switch from:
  - `else if (update.message?.text) { this.handleIncomingMessage(update.message) ... }`
- To:
  - `else if (update.message) { this.handleIncomingMessage(update.message) ... }`
This guarantees photos/documents reach the message handler.

### 4) Refactor handleIncomingMessage to parse text-or-media input
File: `src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts`
- Replace early guard:
  - `if (!msg || !msg.text) return;`
- With media-aware normalization:
  - `if (!msg) return;`
  - `const rawText = String(msg.text || msg.caption || '').trim();`
  - `const hasPhoto = Array.isArray(msg.photo) && msg.photo.length > 0;`
  - `const hasImageDocument = this.isImageDocument(msg.document);`
  - `const hasImage = hasPhoto || hasImageDocument;`
  - Keep `text` variable semantics for command routing: `const text = rawText;`

### 5) Keep command behavior exactly text-first, then route image-aware chat
File: `src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts`
- Existing command blocks (`/start`, `/commands`, `/browse`, etc.) remain text-triggered and unchanged.
- If message has image payload (with or without caption), bypass command-only assumptions and run image-aware call to `handleChat`.

### 6) Inject image attachment context into handleChat caller context
File: `src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts`
- In the main `try` block where `callerContext` is built (around `3215-3225`), append an image context block when `hasImage`:
  - For photo: pick highest-resolution entry (`msg.photo[msg.photo.length - 1]`) file_id.
  - For image document: use `msg.document.file_id`.
  - Resolve file path via `getFile`, download bytes, convert to data URL.
  - Build context section:
    - `INBOUND_TELEGRAM_IMAGE: true`
    - `mime_type`, `size_bytes`, `source` (`photo` or `document`)
    - `data_url` (base64 data URL)
    - `caption_text` if provided
  - Append to `callerContext` passed into `this.deps.handleChat(...)`.

### 7) Preserve journaling/broadcast/session writes for media messages
File: `src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts`
- Where `userMsg` is persisted (`3232+`), keep `content` as:
  - caption text if present,
  - otherwise synthetic placeholder like `[Telegram image received: photo]` / `[Telegram image received: document]`.
- No schema change to `addMessage` required.

### 8) Add graceful failure path for file download errors
File: `src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts`
- If Telegram file fetch fails:
  - send actionable error message to user (`Could not download attached image from Telegram API`) and stop that turn.
  - do not crash polling loop.

## Deterministic behavior after patch
1. Sending a Telegram photo with caption now always reaches `handleIncomingMessage`, downloads the image from Telegram, and calls `handleChat` with image context attached.
2. Sending a Telegram photo without caption still triggers `handleChat` (no longer silently dropped).
3. Sending an image as Telegram document (`mime_type=image/*`) behaves exactly like photo intake.
4. Non-image documents are ignored by image path and continue to behave under existing text/command logic (no regression).
5. Existing text commands (`/start`, `/browse`, `/proposals`, etc.) remain unchanged and deterministic.
6. Poll loop continues robustly after any single media-download failure.

## Acceptance tests
1. **Photo + caption path**
   - Send Telegram photo with caption: `analyze this screenshot`.
   - Expected: bot responds with analysis; logs show update routed through `handleIncomingMessage` and `handleChat` call.
2. **Photo-only path**
   - Send Telegram photo with no caption.
   - Expected: bot still responds (no silent drop), using image context.
3. **Image document path**
   - Send `.png` as document.
   - Expected: same behavior as photo path.
4. **Text command non-regression**
   - Run `/commands`, `/status`, `/browse`.
   - Expected: unchanged command responses.
5. **Failure handling**
   - Force invalid file path or fetch failure (mock/getFile bad ID).
   - Expected: user receives clear error message; polling continues; subsequent text message still works.
6. **Build/type safety**
   - Run `npm run build`.
   - Expected: TypeScript compile passes.

## Risks and compatibility
- **Risk: larger callerContext payload** when embedding base64 image data URL.
  - Mitigation: enforce max image byte threshold before embedding (e.g., reject >6MB with user-facing message).
- **Risk: Telegram API transient failures** on `getFile`/file download.
  - Mitigation: explicit try/catch and non-fatal error path; polling loop unaffected.
- **Compatibility:** backward compatible for all text-only and command-driven Telegram flows; no API contract changes to other routers/modules.

affectedFiles.description: Add Telegram photo/image-document intake, file download helpers, poll-loop routing, and image-aware handleChat context injection

estimatedImpact: Enables native Telegram image-to-Prom workflows (screenshots/photos) with no user workaround, while preserving all existing text command behavior.
executorPrompt: Implement proposal exactly as specified: edit src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts to support inbound Telegram photo and image-document messages end-to-end. Update TelegramUpdate typing, pollLoop routing (message not just text), handleIncomingMessage normalization for text/caption/media, add helper methods for getFile + file download to data URL, and append deterministic inbound-image context block into handleChat callerContext. Preserve existing command flows, add robust media-download error handling, and ensure session history/broadcast still work for media messages. After edits, run npm run build and report results plus a concise test checklist outcome.

    - Returns true for `mime_type` starting with `image/` OR filename extensions `png|jpg|jpeg|webp|gif|bmp`.
  - `private async getTelegramFilePath(fileId: string): Promise<string>`

    - Throws explicit error if missing path.

  - `if (!msg) return;`

1. **Photo + caption path**
